notes: All entities listed as alleged unless a court conviction or guilty plea is noted. Siemens AG and Siemens Argentina pled guilty in Dec 2008. Bribes paid to officials in two presidential administrations, including two presidents. Over $100M in total bribes; $31.3M paid after Mar 2001 when Siemens became a US issuer.

companies.name: Siemens AG
companies.notes: German parent company. Pled guilty Dec 2008 to FCPA conspiracy. Paid $448.5M criminal fine plus $350M SEC disgorgement. Total global settlement ~$1.6B.

companies.name: Siemens S.A. (Argentina)
companies.notes: Argentine subsidiary. Pled guilty Dec 2008 to FCPA violation. Paid $500,000 criminal fine.

intermediaries.name: Carlos Sergi
intermediaries.notes: Led the 'Project Group' of consultants that served as payment intermediaries between Siemens and Argentine officials. Charged by DOJ and SEC (Dec 2011).

intermediaries.name: Miguel Czysch
intermediaries.notes: Served as intermediary and agent of Siemens in the bribe scheme. Used offshore shell companies to disguise and launder funds through fake consulting contracts. Charges pending.

intermediaries.name: Herbert Steffen
intermediaries.notes: Charged by DOJ and SEC (Dec 2011) for role in authorizing and concealing bribe payments.

projects.name: Documento Nacional de Identidad (DNI) Modernization
projects.notes: Contract to replace manually created national identity booklets with state-of-the-art national identity cards for every Argentine citizen. Awarded to Siemens subsidiary in 1998. At least 17 offshore shell companies used to disguise bribe payments through fake consulting contracts.

politicians.name: Argentine Official A (unnamed in DOJ filings)
politicians.notes: SEC complaint references a president who left office in 1999. DOJ filings use coded designations. Bribes allegedly paid to secure the initial DNI contract award.

politicians.name: Argentine Official B (unnamed in DOJ filings)
politicians.notes: DOJ filings reference cabinet ministers in two presidential administrations as bribe recipients. Specific names coded in court documents.

executives_charged.name: Uriel Sharef
executives_charged.position: Member of Siemens AG Central Board
executives_charged.notes: Charged by DOJ and SEC (Dec 2011) for authorizing bribe payments.

executives_charged.name: Andres Truppel
executives_charged.notes: First individual to plead guilty. Admitted to conspiring to funnel ~$100M in bribes, including wiring $7M+ through a New York bank account. Also filed a fraudulent arbitration claim with a false witness statement.

executives_charged.name: Bernd Regendantz
executives_charged.notes: Charged by DOJ and SEC (Dec 2011) for role in concealing bribe payments.
